summary: Phase 10b - Terms acceptance history + grace period + hard-block gate + admin endpoint: 17/17 backend tests pass. Verified GET /api/terms/version exposes new fields (published_at='2026-05-01T00:00:00+00:00', grace_period_days=7, grace_period_ends_at='2026-05-08T00:00:00+00:00', is_hard_block_now bool, changelog with summary_es+summary_en for v2026-05-01); GET /api/terms/me adds grace_period_ends_at, is_hard_block, published_at and respects grace (is_hard_block=False during window); POST /api/terms/accept appends to terms_acceptance_history with version, accepted_at, ip (X-Forwarded-For first hop wins), user_agent (truncated to 255 chars), source ('booking_checkout' honored, default 're_accept'); business owner $push mirrors on db.businesses, manager (is_manager=True) does NOT touch business doc; GET /api/terms/me/history returns {count, history} and {0, []} for legacy accounts; POST /api/auth/register and /api/auth/business/register seed history with source='register' / 'business_register' and a non-empty IP; require_terms_up_to_date helper raises 409 {code:'terms_outdated', current_version, message} when hard-block active and user outdated, returns silently during grace or when up-to-date; bookings.create_booking and businesses.update_my_legal_docs both invoke the gate (verified via source grep); admin endpoint GET /api/admin/users/{user_id}/terms-history returns {user_id, email, full_name, role, current_accepted_version, current_accepted_at, history}, 404 for unknown id, 401/403 unauth.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/terms.py: _hard_block_active uses datetime.fromisoformat on TERMS_VERSION_PUBLISHED_AT; on parse failure it returns True (fail-closed). Reasonable, but consider logging the ValueError so a misconfigured published_at doesn't silently turn the whole platform into hard-block mode.

routers/terms.py accept_terms: source is trimmed to 32 chars and defaults to 're_accept' when payload.source is empty/missing. Spec also lists 'booking_checkout' and 'register'; the API does not validate the value against an allowlist, so any client-supplied string is accepted. Low risk (it's audit metadata) but worth a Literal[...] enum if strict reporting is needed.

routers/terms.py require_terms_up_to_date silently returns when user is not found in db (line 207). That means a stale/deleted user_id in a token never trips the gate. Acceptable today because require_auth already validates the user, but if the auth dependency ever stops doing that lookup, this becomes a bypass.

auth.py register_user / register_business compute IP and user_agent inline and duplicate the dict construction. Consider extracting a helper `_build_terms_entry(request, source)` to share with routers/terms.py - reduces drift if the audit schema grows (e.g., adding session_id, locale).

Migration in server.py was not re-exercised in this run (it only matters on cold-start). The aggregation pipeline filter terms_acceptance_history: {$exists: False} is the right idempotency guard, but if a future seed inserts an empty array [] the filter will skip it and the legacy accepted_terms_version/at won't be backfilled. If that case is possible, change the filter to {$or: [{exists: False}, {$size: 0}]}.

Admin endpoint GET /api/admin/users/{user_id}/terms-history returns the raw history list. For long-lived users this could grow unbounded; consider adding pagination (limit/offset) or sort+slice on the server before LFPDPPP-driven retention is implemented.
test_credentials: JWT minted directly with HS256+JWT_SECRET (admin token uses an existing role:'admin' user discovered in db.users). No TOTP needed because the helper bypasses the /auth/admin/login flow.
seed_data_creation: TEST_F10B_ prefix + RUN uuid: 3 users (client, owner, manager) + 2 businesses + 1 legacy user + 1 register-flow user + 1 business-register user+business. All cleaned up in fixture teardown by id-prefix and email-RUN regex.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_fase10b_history_grace_gate.py is fully isolated and idempotent (TEST_F10B_ prefix + per-run uuid). For gate testing the file monkeypatches routers.terms._hard_block_active in-process (no config change, no restart) and asserts HTTPException directly via asyncio.run on the helper. Bookings/businesses gate wiring is verified via source grep instead of full booking creation to avoid the heavy fixture cost (deposit, services, slots). If the team later wants an end-to-end 409 response from POST /api/bookings, mock create_booking after monkeypatching _hard_block_active and use a freshly-seeded outdated user. Test does NOT exercise the startup migration aggregation pipeline directly - that should be covered in a deployment smoke test if/when TERMS_VERSION is bumped.
